news.title: Haryana RERA initiates suo motu proceedings against Nitara Projects for alleged non-registration
news.para: Haryana RERA's Gurugram bench has initiated suo motu proceedings against Nitara Projects for allegedly developing, marketing and selling residential floors without mandatory registration under the RERA Act. The authority has issued a final opportunity to the promoter to respond, failing which ex-parte proceedings may be initiated.

news.title: Concorde targets ₹650 crore sales in FY27, to deploy ₹200 crore for new projects
news.para: Bengaluru-based developer Concorde is targeting higher sales in FY27 after closing FY26 with sales of about ₹450 crore and recognised revenue of ₹395-400 crore. The company plans to launch 1.5-2 million sq ft during the year, increase its outright land purchase budget and focus largely on mid-segment housing priced between ₹1.5 crore and ₹2.5 crore.

news.title: Knowledge Realty Trust raises ₹500 crore through commercial papers
news.para: Knowledge Realty Trust has raised ₹500 crore through the allotment of listed, rated and redeemable commercial papers on a private placement basis. The commercial papers have a tenure of 91 days from the allotment date of May 12, 2026.

news.title: MMRDA receives three bids to prepare Mumbai metro 14 DPR
news.para: MMRDA is revising the Detailed Project Report for Metro Line 14 connecting Kanjurmarg and Badlapur after failing to attract private developers for a PPP model. The authority is now exploring an EPC route with institutional loans and a deferred payment structure for the ₹18,000 crore project.

news.title: About 5,500 residential, commercial units in Ahmedabad identified as bulk waste generators
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is enforcing new waste management rules for large waste generators. Over 5,500 societies and complexes must process biodegradable waste on-site and segregate dry waste. This aims to reduce landfill pressure and protect the environment. Institutions have one year to comply. Failure to do so will result in strict action.

news.title: Against 3,600 PMAY applicants, only 130 pay 10% margin money in Sambhajinagar
news.para: Slow progress plagues the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, with a mere 130 out of 3,638 applicants submitting the mandatory margin money. To accelerate the scheme, the municipal corporation is now bringing beneficiaries, banks, and developers together. Outreach camps and a dedicated information desk are being established to boost participation and facilitate loan processes for affordable housing.

news.title: Cement companies expect costs, muted demand to dent growth
news.para: Indian cement companies are revising growth expectations downwards. Higher fuel, packaging, and logistics costs are impacting profitability. Companies anticipate a slower demand increase this fiscal year. Price hikes may not fully offset the rising expenses. Some firms are also delaying capacity expansion plans. The West Asia crisis and monsoon forecasts are key concerns.

news.title: Major tenancy reforms implemented in Chandigarh
news.para: Chandigarh has implemented significant tenancy reforms, extending the Assam Tenancy Act, 2021, to replace the outdated 1949 Act. This move aims to formalize the rental market, reduce disputes through mandatory written agreements, and enhance transparency with a dedicated Rent Authority and web portal. The reforms are expected to improve housing utilization and availability for its large tenant population.

news.title: ASK CLAF I invests ₹63 crore for villa project in Arpora, Goa
news.para: ASK Curated Luxury Asset Fund I is investing sixty-three crore rupees in a new villa project in Goa. The project, named Ananda Nilaya, will feature twenty-three villas in Arpora. This development is a partnership with Priority Group. The company anticipates generating three hundred sixty crore rupees in revenue from this venture.

news.title: Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora moves HC challenging his arrest by ED
news.para: Punjab Industries Minister Sanjeev Arora has moved the High Court challenging his recent arrest by the Enforcement Directorate. He claims his detention under the PMLA is illegal and unconstitutional. The ED arrested Arora in connection with a Rs 100 crore GST fraud-linked money laundering case. Arora's plea argues the arrest violates statutory safeguards and constitutional protections.

news.title: Sri Lotus Developers' net profit rises 6.77% to ₹243.30 crore in FY26
news.para: Sri Lotus Developers & Realty reported a 6.77% rise in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹243.30 crore. The company's total income grew significantly by 43.82% to ₹818.71 crore.  Strong pre-sales of ₹1,157 crore, a 137% year-on-year increase, contributed to this robust performance.

news.title: Nexus Select Trust's net operating income up 11% to ₹4.4 billion in Q4 FY26
news.para: Nexus Select Trust reported an 11% year-on-year growth in retail net operating income to ₹4.4 billion in Q4 FY26, declaring a net asset value of ₹164 per unit. Despite a 16.43% decline in net consolidated profit for FY26, the company provided a positive NOI guidance of ₹20.5 – ₹20.7 billion for the upcoming fiscal year.

news.title: NCLT allows withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against Nobility Estates
news.para: The NCLT has allowed withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against Nobility Estates, developer of ATS Group’s Le Grandiose project in Noida, after lenders and the erstwhile management reached a settlement. The tribunal noted that the withdrawal proposal received 92.52% approval from the committee of creditors, including votes from 636 homebuyers, and directed restoration of management control to the company’s board.

news.title: Dubai Holding becomes developer Emaar's top shareholder with $6.5 billion stake
news.para: Dubai Holding has acquired a 22.27% stake in Emaar Properties from the emirate's sovereign wealth fund, becoming its top shareholder with 29.73% ownership. This off-market transaction reshuffles state-linked holdings within Dubai's investment apparatus, reflecting confidence in Emaar's market position and Dubai's economy.

news.title: Berger Paints' net profit declines 4.63% to ₹1,128.02 crore in FY26
news.para: Berger Paints saw its net profit dip by 4.63% in FY26. The company's total income grew by 2.99%. In the fourth quarter, profit after tax surged by 27.52%. Price increases are expected to support margins. The board recommended a dividend of ₹4 per share.

news.title: Welspun One leases 65,000 sq ft at JNPA SEZ to Balmer Lawrie
news.para: Welspun One has entered into a letter of intent with Balmer Lawrie & Co. for 65,000 sq ft of grade A warehousing space at WTC Nhava Sheva, expected to be operational by early 2027. This strategic move will enhance Balmer Lawrie's existing logistics operations within the JNPA SEZ, offering dedicated SEZ warehousing and value-added services to customers.

news.title: Keystone Realtors' net profit declines 30.81% to ₹94.95 crore in FY26
news.para: Keystone Realtors saw its net profit fall by over 30% in FY26. However, its total income increased by nearly 28%. The company achieved strong pre-sales growth and expanded its market share. Collections also saw a significant rise. Keystone Realtors completed six projects during the financial year.
